sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the top rectangle didn't change anything (board_changed: false). Nothing changed. Let me think about this differently.
Let me reconsider the problem from scratch. The board has:
1. Top-left panel: white on top, purple on bottom
2. Main area: white on top, white on bottom
3. Top bar: two colored squares (white and purple) with an indicator
I've been clicking the colored squares and they change the top rectangle in the main area. The indicator moves under the clicked square.
But I can't change the bottom rectangle. Maybe the bottom rectangle is the "target" and the indicator moves under the clicked square.
Wait, maybe the indicator (node 8) is under the white square. The white square is on the left, purple on the right. Maybe the indicator position determines which rectangle to edit. When indicator is under the white square (left), clicking a color changes the top rectangle. When indicator is under the purple square (right), clicking a color changes the bottom rectangle.
But I've been clicking the colored squares themselves. When I click the white square, the indicator moves under white. When I click purple, it moves under purple. So clicking a colored square both colored squares are both controls.
Let me re-examine: the white square is at col 36-38, purple at col 42-44. The indicator is at col 35-39 (under white).
Wait, maybe the indicator isn't under the white square. The indicator is at col 35-39, which is under the white square (col 36-38).
Wait, the indicator is at col 35-39, which is under the white square (col 36-38).
Let me think about this differently. Maybe I need to click on the indicator to move it, then click a colored square to change the selected rectangle.
Actually, let me try clicking on the indicator itself (node 8, around row 7, col 37).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click on the indicator (node 8, around row 7, col 37)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 7, 'col': 37}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
